titleStmt.title: Letter from John Nevin to the Longford Urban District Council [December 1915]
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Nevin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from John Nevin to the Longford Urban District Council concerning the collection of rent in Longford.John Nevin (b. 1866) was Town Sergeant from Longford. The Longford Urban District Council was the towns local authority and was renamed the Longford Town Council in 2002.

correspAction.placeName: Longford Urban District Council, Co. Longford, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Mr Chairman & Gentlemen I beg to report that I have collected rent
for the past month, to the amount of Â£35.1.10
which I lodged to credit of Council as follows:-
Rent Â£29.9.2 and rates Â£5.13.8. The lamps were lighted from the 1st to the 7th
and from the 27th to the 30th, on the 12th there
were 54 lamps out wing to the storm. Your obdt. Servant John Nevin
